content: Hi all, I've been experimenting a bit with writing TCP congestion control algorithms in Rust. They seem to be a pretty well isolated component (you can even do them in BPF) and thus having them in Rust is relatively straightforward (this post are sent through a socket that uses BIC Rust :) ). There is a PR in RFL with the current state, if that sounds interesting and you have some time to spare I'd appreciate some feedback :)
That is some seriously awesome work. I gave a first pass review to some of it
The biggest weird thing to me is having the generic on our version of struct sock. It might be more clear to have a plain sock abstraction, then a wrapper type with the generic
Not sure if you have seen them but the abstractions at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230814092302.1903203-1-dallerivemichele@gmail.com/ may help (cc @Michele Dalle Rive)

Trevor Gross said:

The biggest weird thing to me is having the generic on our version of struct sock. It might be more clear to have a plain sock abstraction, then a wrapper type with the generic

Indeed, that part should be improved. The only issue I see with the approach you suggested it that there will be some thin wrappers around methods of the base wrapper type in the specialized wrapper type. That's usually a pattern I like to minimize.

Trevor Gross said:

Not sure if you have seen them but the abstractions at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230814092302.1903203-1-dallerivemichele@gmail.com/ may help (cc Michele Dalle Rive)

Nikolai Burov (not sure if they are in this Zulip) wrote a nbd implementation on top of the socket abstractions from @Michele Dalle Rive . Since it is exercising the block layer device bindings, I am going to keep it around on top of upstream (Nikolai does not have spare cycles for that). And in turn I will be keeping those socket abstractions alive as well. Perhaps there is potential for some collaboration on that @Valentin Obst?

Not sure if you have seen them but the abstractions at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230814092302.1903203-1-dallerivemichele@gmail.com/ may help (cc @Michele Dalle Rive)

Unfortunately, these abstractions have no overlap with the needs of CCAs. I agree that one should have in mind that, eventually, the abstractions needed by CCAs should fit into the larger context of networking abstractions but currently I would only be extending this patch set without re-using any of the infrastructure that it provides, i.e., not much point in pulling it in :/

And in turn I will be keeping those socket abstractions alive as well. Perhaps there is potential for some collaboration on that @Valentin Obst?

Has there been any follow-up on the idea of a rust-net branch? In that context it would make sense to integrate otherwise disparate abstractions and make it easier to keep the whole thing alive.

Has there been any follow-up on the idea of a rust-net branch? In that context it would make sense to integrate otherwise disparate abstractions and make it easier to keep the whole thing alive.

Happy to set it up like we just did for rust-pci, if someone wants to go for it (or several).

Cc @Davide Rovelli @Amélie Gonzalez too

Miguel Ojeda said:

Happy to set it up like we just did for rust-pci, if someone wants to go for it (or several).

I'd definitely integrate the parts of the CCA abstractions that fit in and help with keeping it up to date.

But there should be someone else that has been around here for a bit longer and is more knowledge in net to take the lead on that branch.
I'm happy to maintain or help maintain a rust-net branch. @Valentin Obst if you are interested, we could co-maintain

Trevor Gross said:

I'm happy to maintain or help maintain a rust-net branch. Valentin Obst if you are interested, we could co-maintain

Yep, I'd be up to help.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Happy to set it up like we just did for rust-pci, if someone wants to go for it (or several).

Cc Davide Rovelli Amélie Gonzalez too

Thanks for looping me in and happy to see that things are moving in this space! Unfortunately I don't have the bandwidth at the moment, I hope to be able to actively join the effort in the near future
Thanks @Trevor Gross @Valentin Obst @Davide Rovelli -- let's start with a rust-net branch then, with Trevor and Valentin then (I invited you to the GitHub organization).
Thanks Miguel! I don't think there is anything to pull in directly but some PRs could probably be rerouted there now (@Michele Dalle Rive)

My pleasure! Things should be set up now:

  • https://rust-for-linux.com/branches#rust-net
  • https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/tree/rust-net

Trevor Gross said:

Thanks Miguel! I don't think there is anything to pull in directly but some PRs could probably be rerouted there now (Michele Dalle Rive)

If you think it's a good idea, I would be happy to open a MR with my abstractions and fix any problem there might be

If you think it's a good idea, I would be happy to open a MR with my abstractions and fix any problem there might be

I would rebase nbd on that :+1:

Yes, I think that would be a good way forward, there we can figure out needed changes (if any) and then pull it in. Thanks!

Nice, perhaps we can also mention that in the readme (of course with the the appropriate disclaimers that it is WIP) once it happens @Trevor Gross?

Same issue on my side, I'm still taking a glance at what goes on, but my research work is taking too much of my time to be as active as I was before, at least for now. Good luck with the net branch :)
